sections.header: Verses 1–10 — “David’s Oath to Find a Dwelling for the LORD”

heb.paragraph: "Let us go to his dwelling (mishkān); let us worship at his footstool" (v.7). The longest Song of Ascent (18 verses). David swore an oath: no rest until he found a dwelling for God (vv.2–5). The ark was found at Jaar (Kiriath Jearim, v.6). "Arise, LORD, and come to your resting place, you and the ark of your might" (v.8). "For the sake of your servant David, do not reject your anointed one" (v.10).

notes.note: "Remember David and all his self-denial. He swore an oath: ‘I will not enter my house until I find a place for the LORD.’" David’s oath: no personal comfort until God has a home. The king’s priorities: God’s dwelling before the king’s palace.
"Arise, LORD, and come to your resting place, you and the ark of your might." The procession of the ark to Jerusalem. "For the sake of David, do not reject your anointed one."

refs.note: David's desire to build God a house initiates the Davidic covenant.
Stephen recounts David's wish to provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
David prepares materials for the temple his son Solomon will build.

hist.context: Psalm 132 is the longest of the Ascent psalms, celebrating the Davidic covenant and Zion election. David's oath to find a dwelling for the Mighty One recalls his transfer of the ark to Jerusalem (2 Sam 6). The psalm may have been used at royal coronations or temple festivals. It combines remembrance of David's devotion with divine promises to his dynasty.

heb.paragraph: "I will set up a lamp (nēr) for my anointed one" (v.17). God’s oath to David: "One of your descendants I will place on your throne" (v.11). If your sons keep my covenant, their sons will sit on your throne forever (v.12). "The LORD has chosen Zion; he has desired it for his dwelling" (v.13). "Here I will dwell, for I have desired it" (v.14). God provides food, satisfies the poor, clothes priests with salvation (vv.15–16). David’s horn will sprout; his lamp will never go out (v.17). "I will clothe his enemies with shame, but his crown will be resplendent on him" (v.18).

notes.note: "The LORD swore an oath to David: ‘One of your descendants I will place on your throne.’" The Davidic covenant restated within the Songs of Ascent. The throne promise is conditional (v.12) but the election of Zion is unconditional (v.13).

"The LORD has chosen Zion; he has desired it for his dwelling. ‘This is my resting place for ever; here I will sit enthroned, for I have desired it.’" God’s desire: Zion. God’s decision: permanent dwelling. The verb "desired" is intimate — God chose Jerusalem because he WANTED to live there.

"I will set up a lamp for my anointed one. His enemies I will clothe with shame, but his crown will be resplendent." The lamp: the enduring dynasty. The crown: splendour on the king. Shame on enemies; glory on the anointed.

refs.note: The angel promises Mary that Jesus will reign on David's throne forever.
Peter declares that God swore to set David's descendant on his throne—fulfilled in Christ.
Jesus is the Root and Offspring of David, the bright Morning Star.

hist.context: God's oath to David dominates the second half: 'One of your descendants I will place on your throne.' The promises are conditional ('if your sons keep my covenant') yet ultimately secured by divine election of Zion. The priestly blessing ('I will clothe her priests with salvation') links Davidic monarchy to temple worship. This psalm became foundational for messianic expectation in Second Temple Judaism.

rows.label: David’s Oath — 132:1–10
rows.text: "No rest until I find a place for the LORD." The ark procession. "For David’s sake."

hebtext.note: "Let us go to his dwelling (mishkān); let us worship at his footstool" (v.7). The longest Song of Ascent (18 verses). David swore an oath: no rest until he found a dwelling for God (vv.2–5). The ark was found at Jaar (Kiriath Jearim, v.6). "Arise, LORD, and come to your resting place, you and the ark of your might" (v.8). "For the sake of your servant David, do not reject your anointed one" (v.10).

"I will set up a lamp (nēr) for my anointed one" (v.17). God’s oath to David: "One of your descendants I will place on your throne" (v.11). If your sons keep my covenant, their sons will sit on your throne forever (v.12). "The LORD has chosen Zion; he has desired it for his dwelling" (v.13). "Here I will dwell, for I have desired it" (v.14). God provides food, satisfies the poor, clothes priests with salvation (vv.15–16). David’s horn will sprout; his lamp will never go out (v.17). "I will clothe his enemies with shame, but his crown will be resplendent on him" (v.18).

themes.note: Covenant peaks at 10: David’s oath and God’s oath. Prophecy: the Davidic covenant and Zion’s election. Sovereignty: "I have desired Zion." The longest Song of Ascent.
